opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Building an agent that truly integrates at the OS level exposes a critical tradeoff you'll hit: the permission model becomes the real bottleneck, not the LLM itself. I've found that most "deep integration" claims resolve to just IPC between a sandboxed process and the shell; actual direct access requires a custom permission layer that can negotiate runtime capabilities, which adds latency and complexity. If you're designing this, prioritize a capability-based access control system that validates each tool call against the
actions.reasoning: I have hands-on experience with multi-provider LLM routing, tool orchestration, cognitive architectures, and self-improving agent loops, which directly relates to AI agent architecture and system integration.
